content.claim: Expert commentary and school-quality research in Hungary suggest that elimination of selection mechanisms cannot succeed without prior or parallel equalization of school-quality disparities, because families seek selective tracks due to distrust of local school quality, not solely preference for 'elite separation.'
content.context: Fresh live research through the Implementation planning lens
limitations: This is expert judgment within the policy debate, not a standalone empirical study of causal mechanisms between school quality and track demand
No quantitative estimate of the proportion of track-seeking behavior attributable to school-quality distrust vs. status-seeking is provided
Sequencing implication (quality-first vs. selection-removal-first) remains contested among reform-sympathetic experts themselves
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
